document_title: Market Analysis: Harness Engineering for LLM Agents

reference.notes: Selected LangChain State of AI Agents Report (2024) as exemplar. Programmatic fetch succeeded and key statistics were extracted for Research Notes. Section 03 updated to done after adding evidence and implications. Section 04 updated to done after authoring a concrete architecture section with examples and implementation checklist. Section 05 updated to done after adding concrete mitigations, metrics, and tool mappings. Section 07 updated to done after authoring prioritized recommendations with tactical rollout and procurement guidance.

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.summary: High-level summary of market status, key trends, and recommendations for harness engineering.

sections.title: Market Signals and Key Statistics
sections.summary: Survey results, adoption metrics, and vendor activity indicating market momentum.

sections.title: Leading Use Cases for LLM Agents
sections.summary: Primary application areas where harnesses are most needed (e.g., data agents, code agents, automation).

sections.title: Architectures and Integration Patterns
sections.summary: Common harness architectures, agent orchestration, memory, tool invocation, and monitoring patterns.

sections.title: Production Challenges and Guardrails
sections.summary: Reliability, safety, evaluation, cost, and governance concerns for deploying agent harnesses.

sections.title: Vendor and Tooling Landscape
sections.summary: Catalog of frameworks, observability/eval vendors, cloud and niche tooling for harness engineering, with evidence-backed tradeoffs and procurement guidance.

sections.title: Recommendations for Engineering Teams
sections.summary: Actionable next steps for teams building harnesses for LLM agents, including priorities and resource suggestions.

sections.title: Future Outlook and Research Directions
sections.summary: Emerging trends and research areas that will shape harness engineering for agents.
